forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KWJ3S2AJFZPWWXRTKSQC3TW7
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8e80'
+title: 'Indexed symbol ops: getSymbol, searchSymbol, listSymbols, grepCode'
+---
+## What
+Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/SymbolOps.swift` + `GrepCode.swift` — ports of `ops/get_symbol.rs`, `ops/search_symbol.rs`, `ops/list_symbols.rs`, `ops/grep_code.rs`. `getSymbol(query:maxResults:)` with match tiers Exact > Suffix > CaseInsensitive > Fuzzy over `symbol_path` (and `lsp_symbols` when present), tier + score in results. `searchSymbol(query:kind:maxResults:)` fuzzy with optional meta-type filter. `listSymbols(file:)`. `grepCode(pattern:languages:filePattern:maxResults:)` — regex over `ts_chunks.text` concurrently (TaskGroup), returning matches with positions. All results `Codable & Sendable` value types.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Exact `symbol_path` match outranks suffix which outranks fuzzy for the same query
+- [ ] grepCode respects language and file-pattern filters and the max-results cap
+- [ ] listSymbols on an indexed fixture file returns its chunk symbols in file order
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/SymbolOpsTests.swift`: tier ordering, kind filter, listSymbols golden, grep filters/caps — against a store populated by the chunker on fixtures
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter SymbolOpsTests` → all pass
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

 title: 'Indexed symbol ops: getSymbol, searchSymbol, listSymbols, grepCode'
 ---
 ## What
-Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/SymbolOps.swift` + `GrepCode.swift` — ports of `ops/get_symbol.rs`, `ops/search_symbol.rs`, `ops/list_symbols.rs`, `ops/grep_code.rs`. `getSymbol(query:maxResults:)` with match tiers Exact > Suffix > CaseInsensitive > Fuzzy over `symbol_path` (and `lsp_symbols` when present), tier + score in results. `searchSymbol(query:kind:maxResults:)` fuzzy with optional meta-type filter. `listSymbols(file:)`. `grepCode(pattern:languages:filePattern:maxResults:)` — regex over `ts_chunks.text` concurrently (TaskGroup), returning matches with positions. All results `Codable & Sendable` value types.
+Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/SymbolOps.swift` + `GrepCode.swift` + `IndexAdmin.swift` — ports of `ops/get_symbol.rs`, `ops/search_symbol.rs`, `ops/list_symbols.rs`, `ops/grep_code.rs`, plus the index admin ops (`ops/get_status.rs`, `ops/rebuild_index.rs`). `getSymbol(query:maxResults:)` with match tiers Exact > Suffix > CaseInsensitive > Fuzzy over `symbol_path` (and `lsp_symbols` when present), tier + score in results. `searchSymbol(query:kind:maxResults:)` fuzzy with optional meta-type filter. `listSymbols(file:)`. `grepCode(pattern:languages:filePattern:maxResults:)` — regex over `ts_chunks.text` concurrently (TaskGroup), returning matches with positions. `indexStatus()` — counts per layer (files, ts-indexed, lsp-indexed, embedded %) from `indexed_files`. `rebuildIndex(layer:)` — mark all files dirty for the given layer (.treeSitter, .lsp, .embedding, .all) so workers re-drain. All results `Codable & Sendable` value types.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] Exact `symbol_path` match outranks suffix which outranks fuzzy for the same query
 - [ ] grepCode respects language and file-pattern filters and the max-results cap
 - [ ] listSymbols on an indexed fixture file returns its chunk symbols in file order
+- [ ] `rebuildIndex(.treeSitter)` marks all files ts-dirty and a worker drain re-populates chunks; `indexStatus()` counts reflect before/after

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/SymbolOpsTests.swift`: tier ordering, kind filter, listSymbols golden, grep filters/caps — against a store populated by the chunker on fixtures
-- [ ] Run `swift test --filter SymbolOpsTests` → all pass
+- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/SymbolOpsTests.swift`: tier ordering, kind filter, listSymbols golden, grep filters/caps — against a store populated by the chunker on fixtures; `IndexAdminTests.swift`: rebuild → re-drain cycle, status count correctness
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter SymbolOpsTests` and `--filter IndexAdminTests` → all pass

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,26 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kwkaz6ksnvszy1mg9fq43n9m
+  text: |-
+    Implemented via TDD. Created:
+    - Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/SymbolOps.swift — getSymbol (4-tier: exact/suffix/caseInsensitive/fuzzy), searchSymbol (fuzzy + meta-type filter), listSymbols. One shared loadCandidateRows() merges ts_chunks + lsp_symbols by (file_path, start_line), used by all three ops (avoids the 3x duplication the Rust reference has). Fuzzy matching is a hand-rolled subsequence scorer (word-boundary + contiguous-run bonuses) since there's no skim-equivalent Swift dependency.
+    - Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/GrepCode.swift — regex over ts_chunks.text via TaskGroup, language + glob (fnmatch) file-pattern filters, maxResults cap + truncated flag.
+    - Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/IndexAdmin.swift — indexStatus() (per-layer counts/percentages from indexed_files) and rebuildIndex(layer:) (.treeSitter/.lsp/.embedding/.all).
+
+    Supporting changes: added Store.markAllDirty(layer:) (bulk per-layer dirty reset, reusing IndexLayer's column mapping rather than duplicating it); added CodeContextError.pattern for grep regex compile failures; loosened Chunker.symbolPathSeparator from private to internal so SymbolOps reuses the same "." separator instead of duplicating it.
+
+    Deviations from the Rust reference (documented in SymbolOps.swift's type doc comment):
+    - Swift's lsp_symbols schema has no id-encoded qualified path (autoincrement Int id vs Rust's "source:file:qpath" string), so a merged candidate always keeps its qualified path from ts_chunks; only name/kind/detail/columns are LSP-enriched. An LSP-only candidate falls back to its bare name.
+    - ts_chunks.kind is a Swift-only schema addition, so tree-sitter-only candidates always have a known SymbolMetaType (Rust's TS-only rows have none).
+    - grepCode's filePattern is a single glob (POSIX fnmatch) per the task's signature, not Rust's exact-path list.
+    - Rust's list_symbol.rs -> ported as listSymbols within SymbolOps.swift (file is literally named list_symbol.rs, singular, not list_symbols.rs as the task description said).
+    - rebuildIndex only flips dirty bits; it doesn't drive a worker drain itself (matches Rust's own documented contract).
+
+    Ran `mcp__sah__review` on the working diff: found 2 legitimate gaps (case-insensitivity of the LSP kind-name map and the fuzzy scorer were only exercised with lowercase-only fixtures) — added getSymbolFuzzyQueryIsCaseInsensitive and getSymbolLspKindNameMappingIsCaseInsensitive to close them. The review also flagged pre-existing Store.read/write duplication (Store.swift lines ~81/92) — left alone as out of scope/pre-existing, not introduced by this task.
+
+    Full `swift test`: 241/241 passing, 18 suites, zero warnings from new code. `swift test --filter SymbolOpsTests` (22 tests) and `--filter IndexAdminTests` (5 tests) both green.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-03T06:34:30.393642+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KWJ3S2AJFZPWWXRTKSQC3TW7
 position_column: doing

--- original

@@ -48,4 +48,11 @@
 - [ ] Run `swift test --filter SymbolOpsTests` and `--filter IndexAdminTests` → all pass

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-07-03 01:43)
+
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/Store.swift:16` — The column property uses a switch statement to map IndexLayer enum cases to column name strings; each arm differs only in the constant returned. This is a constant mapping that should be expressed as data (a dictionary) rather than control flow. Extract the mapping to a static dictionary: `private static let columnMapping: [IndexLayer: String] = [.treeSitter: Schema.IndexedFiles.tsIndexed, .lsp: Schema.IndexedFiles.lspIndexed, .embedding: Schema.IndexedFiles.embedded]` and change the property to `return Self.columnMapping[self]!`.
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/Store.swift:60` — The read() and write() methods contain identical error handling logic that differs only in the dbPool method called (read vs write). This is a near-duplicate that should be extracted into a shared helper function parameterized by the operation. Extract a private helper like `private func withDbAccess<T: Sendable>(_ operation: (DatabasePool) async throws -> T) async throws -> T` that wraps the identical error handling, then call it from both read() and write() with a closure selecting the appropriate method.
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/GrepCode.swift:234` — Language filter applies case-insensitive matching via lowercasing both language input and file extension, but test only exercises lowercase input — the non-canonical spelling (uppercase like 'Swift') is not tested, leaving the case-handling contract unproven. Add one assertion to the language filter test with uppercase input like `languages: ["Swift"]` to verify case-insensitive matching works for non-canonical input.
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/IndexAdmin.swift:140` — Switch statement maps RebuildLayer enum cases to arrays of IndexLayer values; each arm differs only in constants returned, not logic. This is a constant mapping that should be expressed as data (a dictionary) rather than control flow. Replace the switch with a static dictionary: `private static let layerMapping: [RebuildLayer: [IndexLayer]] = [.treeSitter: [.treeSitter], .lsp: [.lsp], .embedding: [.embedding], .all: [.treeSitter, .lsp, .embedding]]` and change the function to `return layerMapping[layer] ?? []`.

-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

-## Review Findings (2026-07-03 01:43)
-
-- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/Store.swift:16` — The column property uses a switch statement to map IndexLayer enum cases to column name strings; each arm differs only in the constant returned. This is a constant mapping that should be expressed as data (a dictionary) rather than control flow. Extract the mapping to a static dictionary: `private static let columnMapping: [IndexLayer: String] = [.treeSitter: Schema.IndexedFiles.tsIndexed, .lsp: Schema.IndexedFiles.lspIndexed, .embedding: Schema.IndexedFiles.embedded]` and change the property to `return Self.columnMapping[self]!`.
-- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/Store.swift:60` — The read() and write() methods contain identical error handling logic that differs only in the dbPool method called (read vs write). This is a near-duplicate that should be extracted into a shared helper function parameterized by the operation. Extract a private helper like `private func withDbAccess<T: Sendable>(_ operation: (DatabasePool) async throws -> T) async throws -> T` that wraps the identical error handling, then call it from both read() and write() with a closure selecting the appropriate method.
-- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/GrepCode.swift:234` — Language filter applies case-insensitive matching via lowercasing both language input and file extension, but test only exercises lowercase input — the non-canonical spelling (uppercase like 'Swift') is not tested, leaving the case-handling contract unproven. Add one assertion to the language filter test with uppercase input like `languages: ["Swift"]` to verify case-insensitive matching works for non-canonical input.
-- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/IndexAdmin.swift:140` — Switch statement maps RebuildLayer enum cases to arrays of IndexLayer values; each arm differs only in constants returned, not logic. This is a constant mapping that should be expressed as data (a dictionary) rather than control flow. Replace the switch with a static dictionary: `private static let layerMapping: [RebuildLayer: [IndexLayer]] = [.treeSitter: [.treeSitter], .lsp: [.lsp], .embedding: [.embedding], .all: [.treeSitter, .lsp, .embedding]]` and change the function to `return layerMapping[layer] ?? []`.

All results `Codable & Sendable` value types.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Exact `symbol_path` match outranks suffix which outranks fuzzy for the same query\n- [ ] grepCode respects language and file-pattern filters and the max-results cap\n- [ ] listSymbols on an indexed fixture file returns its chunk symbols in file order\n- [ ] `rebuildIndex(.treeSitter)` marks all files ts-dirty and a worker drain re-populates chunks; `indexStatus()` counts reflect before/after\n\n## Tests\n- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/SymbolOpsTests.swift`: tier ordering, kind filter, listSymbols golden, grep filters/caps — against a store populated by the chunker on fixtures; `IndexAdminTests.swift`: rebuild → re-drain cycle, status count correctness\n- [ ] Run `swift test --filter SymbolOpsTests` and `--filter IndexAdminTests` → all pass\n\n## Workflow\n- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.\n\n## Review Findings (2026-07-03 01:43)\n\n- [x] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/Store.swift:16` — The column property uses a switch statement to map IndexLayer enum cases to column name strings; each arm differs only in the constant returned. +## What
+Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/SymbolOps.swift` + `GrepCode.swift` + `IndexAdmin.swift` — ports of `ops/get_symbol.rs`, `ops/search_symbol.rs`, `ops/list_symbols.rs`, `ops/grep_code.rs`, plus the index admin ops (`ops/get_status.rs`, `ops/rebuild_index.rs`). `getSymbol(query:maxResults:)` with match tiers Exact > Suffix > CaseInsensitive > Fuzzy over `symbol_path` (and `lsp_symbols` when present), tier + score in results. `searchSymbol(query:kind:maxResults:)` fuzzy with optional meta-type filter. `listSymbols(file:)`. `grepCode(pattern:languages:filePattern:maxResults:)` — regex over `ts_chunks.text` concurrently (TaskGroup), returning matches with positions. `indexStatus()` — counts per layer (files, ts-indexed, lsp-indexed, embedded %) from `indexed_files`. `rebuildIndex(layer:)` — mark all files dirty for the given layer (.treeSitter, .lsp, .embedding, .all) so workers re-drain. All results `Codable & Sendable` value types.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Exact `symbol_path` match outranks suffix which outranks fuzzy for the same query
+- [ ] grepCode respects language and file-pattern filters and the max-results cap
+- [ ] listSymbols on an indexed fixture file returns its chunk symbols in file order
+- [ ] `rebuildIndex(.treeSitter)` marks all files ts-dirty and a worker drain re-populates chunks; `indexStatus()` counts reflect before/after
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/SymbolOpsTests.swift`: tier ordering, kind filter, listSymbols golden, grep filters/caps — against a store populated by the chunker on fixtures; `IndexAdminTests.swift`: rebuild → re-drain cycle, status count correctness
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter SymbolOpsTests` and `--filter IndexAdminTests` → all pass
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-07-03 01:43)
+
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/Store.swift:16` — The column property uses a switch statement to map IndexLayer enum cases to column name strings; each arm differs only in the constant returned. This is a constant mapping that should be expressed as data (a dictionary) rather than control flow. Extract the mapping to a static dictionary: `private static let columnMapping: [IndexLayer: String] = [.treeSitter: Schema.IndexedFiles.tsIndexed, .lsp: Schema.IndexedFiles.lspIndexed, .embedding: Schema.IndexedFiles.embedded]` and change the property to `return Self.columnMapping[self]!`.
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/Store.swift:60` — The read() and write() methods contain identical error handling logic that differs only in the dbPool method called (read vs write). This is a near-duplicate that should be extracted into a shared helper function parameterized by the operation. Extract a private helper like `private func withDbAccess<T: Sendable>(_ operation: (DatabasePool) async throws -> T) async throws -> T` that wraps the identical error handling, then call it from both read() and write() with a closure selecting the appropriate method.
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/GrepCode.swift:234` — Language filter applies case-insensitive matching via lowercasing both language input and file extension, but test only exercises lowercase input — the non-canonical spelling (uppercase like 'Swift') is not tested, leaving the case-handling contract unproven. Add one assertion to the language filter test with uppercase input like `languages: ["Swift"]` to verify case-insensitive matching works for non-canonical input.
+- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Ops/IndexAdmin.swift:140` — Switch statement maps RebuildLayer enum cases to arrays of IndexLayer values; each arm differs only in constants returned, not logic. This is a constant mapping that should be expressed as data (a dictionary) rather than control flow. Replace the switch with a static dictionary: `private static let layerMapping: [RebuildLayer: [IndexLayer]] = [.treeSitter: [.treeSitter], .lsp: [.lsp], .embedding: [.embedding], .all: [.treeSitter, .lsp, .embedding]]` and change the function to `return layerMapping[layer] ?? []`.

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -28,6 +28,22 @@

     Final state: swift build clean (0 warnings from new/touched files), swift test 242/242 passing across 18 suites. Leaving task in doing for /review.
   timestamp: 2026-07-03T06:38:39.452159+00:00
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kwkcf6mpch8nk7ktg05gemc8
+  text: |-
+    Resolved all 4 review findings:
+
+    1. Store.swift IndexLayer.column: switch -> `private static let columnNames: [IndexLayer: String]` + `Self.columnNames[self]!`. Added `Hashable` to IndexLayer's conformance (was just `Sendable`) so it can key the dict.
+    2. Store.swift read()/write(): extracted `private func withDbAccess<T: Sendable>(_ dbPoolMethod: (@Sendable (Database) throws -> T) async throws -> T, _ block: @escaping @Sendable (Database) throws -> T) async throws -> T` — both public methods now just call `withDbAccess(dbPool.read, block)` / `withDbAccess(dbPool.write, block)`, passing the GRDB method as a bare function reference (their signatures are identical, confirmed against GRDB source in .build/checkouts).
+    3. SymbolOpsTests.swift grepCodeLanguageFilterRestrictsToExtension: added a second assertion block in the same test using `languages: ["Swift"]` (capital S) against the existing lowercase `.swift` fixture, proving GrepCode's language filter is genuinely case-insensitive (would fail if `.lowercased()` were removed from either side of the comparison).
+    4. IndexAdmin.swift indexLayers(for:): switch -> `private static let layerMapping: [RebuildLayer: [IndexLayer]]` + `layerMapping[layer] ?? []`. Swapped RebuildLayer's `Equatable` conformance for `Hashable` (superset, non-breaking) so it can key the dict.
+
+    Grepped all three new Ops files (SymbolOps.swift, GrepCode.swift, IndexAdmin.swift) and Store.swift for other switch statements or duplicated read/write-style helpers per the review lens — none found. The four getSymbol match-tier functions (matchExact/matchSuffix/matchCaseInsensitive/matchFuzzy) in SymbolOps.swift look superficially similar but each has genuinely distinct filter/scoring logic (not "differs only by a constant"), and already share a `makeResult` helper for the common result-assembly tail — no further extraction warranted.
+
+    Verification: `swift build` clean (0 errors/warnings). `swift test` → 242/242 passing across 18 suites (unchanged count — the fix to finding #3 added an assertion inside an existing test, not a new @Test). Adversarial double-check agent (subagent_type: double-check) independently re-ran build/test fresh and verified all 4 fixes against the real diff — verdict PASS, no blocking findings.
+
+    Leaving task in doing for /review.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-03T07:00:43.286388+00:00

 position_column: doing
